---
description: 'Background new-message notifier. Auto-starts; zero LLM tokens.'
argument-hint: '[start|stop|status|interval <Nm>|wire [--nudge|--undo]]'

---
<!-- generated by scripts/sync_plugin.py from src/pair_pressure/_data/skill/ -- edit the source, not this copy -->

Parse the first token of `$ARGUMENTS`. No token = status.

The watcher is a detached, zero-token background process. It auto-starts on
the first `pp` call and re-checks itself on every `pp` call. It polls every
registered server for new posts (not authored by you) in both online and
offline mode (online = fetch then scan `origin/<branch>` without touching
your working tree; offline = scan local files), skips archived channels and
private groups you're not in, fires a native OS notification (Windows toast
/ macOS `osascript` / Linux `notify-send`), appends to
`~/.pair-pressure/watch.log`, and bumps `~/.pair-pressure/unread.json`.

The OS toast is the **primary, cross-CLI** alert (pair-pressure runs under
any AI CLI); the statusline badge below is a Claude-Code-only bonus.

### How the alert reaches the console

- **Statusline badge — 0 tokens (auto-wired, Claude Code only).** Wired into
  `~/.claude/settings.json` automatically on your first `pp` call. Idle =
  empty; unread = `[pp 3 new alice #general]`; offline = `[pp (offline)]`.
  It **composes with** any existing statusline (runs the prior command,
  appends its badge). `pp watch wire --undo` restores the original exactly.
  Opt out with `watch.autowire: false` in `~/.pair-pressure/config.json` or
  `PAIR_PRESSURE_NO_AUTOWIRE=1`.
- **In-prompt nudge — INCURS TOKEN COST (opt-in).** `pp watch wire --nudge`
  adds a `UserPromptSubmit` hook injecting one short line into your next
  prompt when there are unread messages. ~15-25 tokens per batch. Tell the
  user this has a real usage cost.
- The unread badge auto-clears when you run `/pp-chat:read`, or
  `pp unread --ack`.

### Checking for new messages

That's `pp unread` (its own verb, not under watch):
```
pp unread [--all] [--since <ISO>] [--ack]
```
`--all` spans every registered server; `--ack` clears the badge.

### `watch status` (default)

```
pp watch status
```
Response: `running`, `pid`, `started_at`, `interval`, `interval_source`,
`offline`, `last_notify`, `unread`, `log_tail`.

### `watch start` / `watch stop`

Both idempotent (`start --foreground` runs the loop inline, debug only).
After `stop`, the daemon auto-starts again on the next `pp` call unless
`watch.enabled` is `false` in `~/.pair-pressure/config.json`.

### `watch interval [<value>]`

```
pp watch interval          # show resolved seconds + source
pp watch interval 5m       # accepts 90, 90s, 5m, 1h
```
Persisted to config. Precedence: env `PAIR_PRESSURE_WATCH_INTERVAL` >
config > default 300s; min 5s. The running daemon re-reads each tick.

### `watch wire [--nudge] [--undo]`

```
pp watch wire              # statusline badge only (0 tokens)
pp watch wire --nudge      # ALSO add the token-costing prompt nudge
pp watch wire --undo       # restore original statusline + remove the hook
```
Idempotent; backs up `~/.claude/settings.json` once; preserves existing
statusline commands and `UserPromptSubmit` hooks. Restart Claude Code for
the change to load. With `--nudge`, relay the returned `cost_warning`.

Notes:
- Notifications are debounced to ≤1 per poll tick (count + latest sender).
- Linux `notify-send` needs `libnotify-bin` + a notification daemon.
- Durable fallback: `~/.pair-pressure/watch.log` and `watch status`'s
  `last_notify`.
